The [[Orlean]] '''Star Cab''' was an exploration vessel considered ancient by the time of the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

==Characteristics==
Star Cabs originated early enough in galactic history that [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] units were not yet completely reliable. A Star Cab's Class 10 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] was much slower than those employed by most [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] scouts; indeed, by that era a Class 10 would have been a more common speed for a backup hyperdrive, which the Star Cab lacked entirely. Star Cabs included a complement of [[Droid/Legends|droids]] that handled the day-to-day operation of the vessel; the standard navigation droid equipped on Star Cabs may have been an ancient predecessor to the [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]]. Star Cabs were usually heavily armed, considering the wilder state of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in their heyday. Their standard armament consisted of four [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]], two firing forward and two aft.<ref name="GG8" />

==Role==
The Star Cab was employed primarily as a scout vessel, usually by veteran scouts.<ref name="GG8" />

==History==
[[Scout/Legends|Scouts]] who served aboard Star Cabs for extended periods were said to develop disagreeable attitudes toward the ships' droid crews.<ref name="GG8" /> [[Luskin Exovar]] kept a Star Cab hanging from the ceiling of his [[Exovar's Emporium|emporium]] along with several other vehicles.<ref name="WHSV">''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade's]] ship in ''[[Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand 1]]'' strongly resembles a Star Cab, and is approximately the same size. However, it houses a [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunter]] in a small ventral hangar; [[Galaxy Guide 8: Scouts|''Galaxy Guide 8'']] does not indicate whether Star Cabs had hangars.
